objects.name: Analytic 1711
objects.description: The defender correlates foreground service start or promotion activity with persistent-notification presentation, long-lived application execution, and continued access to while-in-use sensors or network activity outside expected user-driven context. The analytic looks for an application invoking foreground service APIs, sustaining a foreground state longer than expected for its declared role, and retaining camera, microphone, location, or other sensor access while the device is locked, the app lacks recent interaction, or the notification identity/function does not match the application’s behavior.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application calls startForegroundService() or startForeground() / ServiceCompat.startForeground() and transitions to persistent foreground-service execution at the start of the chain

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Persistent foreground-service notification is created, updated, or remains visible while app behavior or notification identity is inconsistent with declared function during the persistence interval

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Foreground service continues accessing camera, microphone, location, or other while-in-use sensors after service promotion and outside recent user interaction

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Apps legitimately expected to run foreground services such as navigation, fitness, calling, media playback, enterprise VPN, accessibility, or device-management apps
Approved foreground service types and role-to-type mappings, especially for Android 14+ and later
Duration a foreground service may legitimately remain active before suspicion increases
Maximum expected delay between service promotion and sensor activation for legitimate workflows
Patterns indicating misleading or impersonating foreground notifications, such as benign-looking text or mismatched app function
How recently the user must have interacted with the app for sensor or network activity to be considered expected
Minimum sustained outbound volume or beacon frequency during persistent foreground execution
